results.title: Cristiano Ronaldo Leaves Social Media Influencer In Tears With Touching Words During Special Meeting Ahead Of FIFA World Cup 2026 Match | Video
results.description: Ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Cristiano Ronaldo met Belgian influencer Celine Dept at Portugal’s training base. Emotional after finally meeting her idol, Celine was comforted by Ronaldo’s warm and humble response. The touching interaction went viral, earning praise from fans and showcasing Ronaldo’s kindness and genuine connection with supporters.

results.title: Cristiano Ronaldo sends heartwarming message for fans ahead of Portugal’s 2026 FIFA World Cup opener
results.description: Cristiano Ronaldo has won domestic leagues in England, Spain, and Italy, a Champions League five times, and the European Championship with Portugal. The one trophy that has eluded him across two decades of professional football is the only one that would put the GOAT debate to rest. At 41, in what is almost certainly his final World Cup, he arrived in the United States dressed in a navy-grey team jacket and fashionable glasses, telling reporters to take it one match at a time.

results.title: Amazon India’s two-wheeler store clocks 2x YoY growth, tier 2-3 cities lead
results.description: New Delhi [India], June 12 (ANI): Amazon India’s two-wheeler store on Amazon.in has recorded 2x year-on-year growth, largely driven by Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, as consumers increasingly choose premium and electric bikes alongside commuter models, according to a statement by Amazon.in. Aman Lohan, Director – Home, Kitchen and Outdoors, Amazon India, said, “The two-wheeler buying journey in India is often long and can get overwhelming with multiple options, fuel types and price points to consider.

results.title: Port Jefferson boys baseball season ends against Tuckahoe in Class C regional final
results.description: By Charlie Zeeve Port Jefferson High School ran headlong into a Division I–bound ace with his best stuff and fell, 2-0, to Tuckahoe in Saturday’s state Class C regional final at Somers High School. The Royals put traffic on the bases throughout, but Tigers pitcher Connor Brice piled up 14 strikeouts and stranded runners in each of the first three innings. Ben Amadio notched one of Port Jeff’s two hits, and the Royals worked five walks, but never found the one swing to flip the game. Tuckahoe broke through in the bottom of the third when Jay Romeo flared a two-out single to center to score the opening run. The Tigers added insurance in the sixth with a perfectly executed squeeze bunt, and their Villanova-bound right-hander closed the door from there, striking out the final eight Royals to end it. The challenge began before the first pitch. Port Jefferson’s arrival at the wrong field delayed the scheduled 11 a.m. start until shortly after noon, and by game time temperatures had climbed into the 90s. The heat was made even more intense by the artificial turf at Somers High School, creating taxing conditions for both teams throughout the afternoon. “It sounds like a pretty basic thing to say, but we are incredibly proud of this group,” said head coach Jesse Rosen. “Being around them for the last three plus months is enjoyable, whether you’re a kid on the team, whether you’re an adult in charge of the team, or whatever else, it really was. The vibe, the feel of the group was hardworking with an intent on winning a county championship and looking to go beyond that.” “Luckily, we accomplished goal number one and fell a little bit short of the next step, but I can say that on a daily basis, when you look at the greater outcome of things, we really did feel like we were making progress, getting better,” Rosen continued. “Whether it was identifying base running, identifying different aspects of defense that we worked on – I’m proud of our progress. We obviously saw a kid with a high quality arm, and sometimes you run into that and it gets the better of us. That is certainly what happened today, but [I am] beyond proud of our season.” The loss caps a strong spring for Port Jefferson, which battled through the county bracket to claim the Suffolk Class C title and earned its shot at the regional crown. With a core that embraced tough pitching and tight, late-inning games, the Royals’ run ends one round shy of the state final four, but with a standard re-set for what comes next.
